cards.hook: Two AI labs are pulling away. Three are stalling.
cards.caption: Two labs are pulling away. Three are stalling. Frontier release cadence, actually charted with methodology. This is the signal my feed keeps while the timeline argues vibes.
cards.stance: Release cadence, not benchmarks, is the real leaderboard now.

cards.hook: Meta is shipping a commercial AI model to catch its rivals.
cards.caption: Meta is going commercial to catch OpenAI and Google. The open-weights champion wants your money now. Caught this before the timeline had takes on it.
cards.stance: Meta abandoning free-and-open was inevitable the moment it fell behind.

cards.hook: Every 2024 AI model release on one scrollable timeline.
cards.caption: Every 2024 AI model release on one scrollable timeline. The year the pace broke your brain, laid out flat. The kind of thing my feed surfaces before you go looking.

cards.hook: Alibaba says its new model beats DeepSeek.
cards.caption: Alibaba says its new model beats DeepSeek. China's model war just got a second front, and the West isn't in this fight. The release my feed flagged before the timeline noticed.
cards.stance: The real frontier race is now Alibaba vs DeepSeek, not OpenAI vs Google.

cards.hook: OpenAI delayed a model release after a request from the Trump administration.
cards.caption: OpenAI staggered its GPT-5.6 preview after a Trump administration request. Model releases are national policy now. Found this before the timeline turned it into a war.
cards.stance: Frontier releases now clear political review before they clear you.

cards.hook: Grok predicts AI takeoff with 4-7 more algorithmic leaps.
cards.caption: Grok says takeoff is close: 4-7 more algorithmic OOMs, then AI automates its own research. Math, code, robotics compound from there. The kind of take my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Recursive self-improvement isn't sci-fi anymore, it's a roadmap.

cards.hook: Altman: OpenAI's next big model, Orion, ships by December.
cards.caption: Altman teased it: Orion, OpenAI's next big model, lands by December. The Q4 release everyone will be arguing about. You saw it here before the timeline did.

cards.hook: Meta's new model transcribes + translates 100 languages
cards.caption: Meta's new model transcribes AND translates close to 100 languages. One model, not a dozen. The releases that matter get buried in an hour — this is the stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The multilingual arms race is where the next moat is, not chatbots.

cards.hook: A bot swarm scraped GitHub for AI training data
cards.caption: For 12 hours GitHub got hammered by a massive bot/AI scraping attack. Everyone's code. Your code. The training-data land grab is getting ugly — found this before the timeline caught up.
cards.stance: Every public repo is already someone's training set. You just weren't told.

cards.hook: She broke the AI-sentience story 5 months before ChatGPT
cards.caption: Four years ago she broke the Blake Lemoine 'sentient AI' story — 5 months before ChatGPT existed. Now Anthropic, Google and Meta hire scientists to study it. The threads that age well, kept.
cards.stance: The guy everyone mocked in 2022 was early, not wrong.

cards.hook: Companies are making Claude talk like a caveman to save tokens
cards.caption: Companies are tuning AI to talk like a terse caveman, not a polite chatbot — on purpose, to stop burning tokens. Every 'please' costs money now. The weird AI economics my feed catches.
cards.stance: Politeness in prompts is a line item, and it's getting cut.

cards.hook: The US government asked OpenAI to slow down a release
cards.caption: The US government asked OpenAI to stagger an AI model release. Regulators are now in the launch calendar. This is where the real fight moves — caught before the timeline noticed.
cards.stance: Model release dates are becoming a policy negotiation, not an engineering call.

cards.hook: Meta built an AI model that grades other AI models' work
cards.caption: Meta released an AI model whose job is checking other AI models' work. AI reviewing AI. The self-supervising loop starts here — the releases my feed keeps before they scroll away.
cards.stance: The next models won't be trained by humans — they'll be graded by other models.

cards.hook: AI sovereignty is now a hardware problem, not a software one
cards.caption: AI sovereignty just became a hardware problem — Huawei's 100x scaling roadmap signals who owns the chips owns the models. The economics story under the hype, kept by my feed.
cards.stance: Whoever controls the silicon controls the AI, and it isn't the US by default.

cards.hook: Google's Genie can generate games. Game stocks just dropped.
cards.caption: Google's Genie can generate playable worlds from a prompt — and videogame stocks slid the same day. The market is pricing in AI. Found before the timeline caught it.
cards.stance: Generative game models are a bigger threat to studios than anyone's admitting.

cards.hook: Alibaba just dropped 100+ open-source AI models at once.
cards.caption: Alibaba shipped over 100 new open-source AI models in one move — text, image, the works. The open-weight race isn't slowing down. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The best open models won't come from the West.

cards.hook: NASA and IBM built an AI model for weather and climate.
cards.caption: NASA teamed with IBM Research on a foundation model for weather and climate — open science, real forecasts. AI for the planet, not just chatbots. Found before the timeline.

cards.hook: White House just lifted its export ban on Anthropic's AI.
cards.caption: Weeks after freezing foreign access, the White House reversed course on exporting Anthropic's advanced models. AI policy is whiplash right now. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: AI export controls are being written and rewritten faster than the models ship.

cards.hook: Alibaba's Qwen models target Meta's Llama 2 directly.
cards.caption: Qwen-7B and Qwen-7B-Chat dropped to rival Llama 2 — 21k stars and climbing. The open-weight fight is getting crowded. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Llama's open-source lead is thinner than Meta wants you to think.

cards.hook: The White House may vet AI models before you can use them.
cards.caption: A proposed rule would have the government review new AI models before public release. Pre-launch approval for code. What that means for open source is the real question. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Government pre-review of models is a soft kill switch for open source.

cards.hook: A new AI reads stress from your brain at 82.78% accuracy.
cards.caption: A Riemannian Manifold Attention Network hit 82.78% accuracy detecting stress from EEG signals — beating prior benchmarks. Brain-reading AI is quietly getting good. Found before the timeline.

cards.hook: You've been renting your AI. This is what owning it looks like.
cards.caption: Osaurus runs local models: no account, no API key, no Electron, fully open source. You own the whole stack. This is the stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: If your AI needs an API key, you don't own it — you're renting.

cards.hook: The White House is writing rules for AI models
cards.caption: The White House is writing rules for AI models. Whatever ships next won't just be a release, it'll be a policy fight. The stuff my feed catches before the timeline does.
cards.stance: AI standards from the government will shape releases more than any lab does.

cards.hook: Bettors give GPT-5.6 a 90% chance to ship by July 31
cards.caption: Polymarket gives GPT-5.6 a 90% chance to drop by July 31, but only 1% before June 30. The market is timing your next model before OpenAI announces it. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Prediction markets now leak release dates faster than the labs do.

cards.hook: The US-China frontier model gap is down to 2.7%
cards.caption: Stanford's 2026 AI Index says the US-China frontier gap has effectively closed, down to ~2.7% and Chinese models now trade the lead. The race you thought was settled isn't. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: The US lead in frontier models is basically a rounding error now.

cards.hook: Frontier models now ship monthly, like continuous deployment
cards.caption: Monthly frontier releases mean AI has stopped shipping versions and started shipping like CI/CD. Blink and your stack is a generation behind. The stuff my feed keeps so you don't miss a drop.
cards.stance: Model releases are now a deploy cadence, not an event.
